title: capraria biflora
intro: Capraria biflora, also known as Two-flowered Hawk's-beard, is a perennial herb native to the Mediterranean region, characterized by its clusters of small, white flowers and slender leaves. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antispasmodic properties. Its medicinal properties are believed to provide various health benefits, including relief from respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as aiding in the treatment of digestive problems and skin conditions. Capraria biflora can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in folk remedies to calm the nervous system and promote relaxation.
